title: fix: prevent catastrophic backtracking in dart function extractor regex
author: AvoMandjian

stage1.summary: The PR converts non-possessive quantifiers to possessive quantifiers (++ and *+) in the Dart function declaration regex. The backtracking problem is real: the annotation sub-pattern `(?:@[\w\.\(\),<>\s]+)\s*` has overlapping whitespace consumption between the character class (which includes \s) and the trailing \s*, and wrapping in `()*` creates exponential backtracking on inputs with multiple @-prefixed tokens that don't ultimately match. Tested: 25 @-params causes the original regex to hang indefinitely while the possessive version completes in microseconds. Additionally, the possessive version actually fixes a latent correctness bug: the original regex would backtrack through annotations like @override and produce garbage single-character function names (e.g., 'd' instead of 'build' for `@override Widget build(...)`). The possessive version correctly captures the real function name in those cases. Requires Python >=3.11 which is already the project minimum.
